title: euphorbia greenwayi subsp. breviaculeata
intro: Euphorbia greenwayi subsp. breviaculeata, also known as the dwarf greenway euphorbia, is a succulent plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its rich concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Euphorbia greenwayi subsp. breviaculeata include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, with the roots and stems being the primary sources of medicinal compounds.
